name: Dr. Rehan Mahmood
headline: Associate Professor | Director of SSTRL | CubeSat / Lunar Mission / Satellite Communications
roles: Former Visiting Consultant, Sultan Qaboos University
bio:
Dr. Rehan Mahmood is an aerospace systems engineer, academic, and project director based in Pakistan, with over 20 years of experience in satellite technology and communication engineering. He is Director and Principal Investigator of the Small Satellite Technology and Research Lab (SSTRL) and Associate Professor at the Institute of Space Technology (IST), Islamabad. His work spans satellite communications, CubeSat systems, laboratory establishment, engineering education, and technical program leadership.

He is widely recognized for his role in Pakistan's lunar CubeSat mission ICUBE-Q, launched aboard Chang'e-6, and for sustained contributions to small-satellite capability development through ICUBE-1, SSS-2A, ICUBE-N, and ICUBE-CSAT. His current work also includes NGN/NTN–PPDR digital-twin and scenario-evaluation research, linking space systems with resilient communications and telecom-space convergence.
